hub_subtitle: Conversação para viagens, listening de verdade e reuniões internacionais — rumo a Nova York, setembro de 2026

lesson.menu_title: Getting Around and Getting Things Done -- Taxis, Subways, Restaurants, and Shops
lesson.menu_desc: A cidade em movimento: táxi, metrô, diner e loja na Fifth Avenue -- 37 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 3 -- Getting Around and Getting Things Done
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Lucimara | Lesson 3 -- Getting Around and Getting Things Done

listenings.text: Ladies and gentlemen, this is the downtown 6 train to Brooklyn Bridge. The next stop is Grand Central, Forty-Second Street. Transfer is available to the 4, the 5, the 7, and the S shuttle. Please step all the way into the car and stand clear of the closing doors. Due to construction this weekend, the 6 train is running express from Fifty-Ninth Street to Fourteenth Street. If you need a local stop, please take the 4 or the 5 across the platform. It is rush hour, so the next three trains will be crowded, but there is another train directly behind this one. Again, the next stop is Grand Central, Forty-Second Street. Please watch the gap between the train and the platform.

Hi there, welcome in. Sit anywhere you like. My name is Rachel and I will be taking care of you today. Let me run through a couple of things before you order. The breakfast menu runs all day here, so if you want pancakes at four in the afternoon, that is completely fine. Today we have two specials: the soup of the day is tomato, and the sandwich of the day is turkey with avocado, and both of them come with fries or a salad. Now, two things people always ask me. First, the prices on the menu do not include sales tax, so the number on the check will be a little higher than what you read. Second, the tip is not included either, so if you were happy with everything, twenty percent is the normal amount in New York. And if you are in a hurry, just tell me, and I will make it a to-go order. I will give you a minute with the menu.

items.situation: You are on Fifth Avenue with two heavy bags. Stop a taxi and tell the driver where you are going.
tips: Raise your hand and hail a cab: "Taxi!"
The Lucerne Hotel, 79th and Amsterdam, please.

items.situation: The driver says the fare will be "about thirty-two fifty" and the street is loud. Make sure you got it.
tips: Sorry, it's loud out here. Let me read that back to you: thirty-two fifty. Is that right?
And is the tip included, or do I add it on the screen?

items.situation: You sit down at a diner on Ninth Avenue. Order lunch for one, and find out what is good today.
tips: Could I get a table for one, please?
What's the soup of the day?

items.situation: You have finished lunch and you are in a hurry. Get the check and pay.
tips: Could I have the check, please? Is the tip included?
Do you take credit cards? And could I have a receipt?

items.situation: In a store on Fifth Avenue you like a coat, but you are not sure about the size.
tips: Could I try this on? Where is the fitting room?
Do you have this in a medium?

items.situation: At the register, the number is higher than the price on the tag. Ask why, and take proof that you paid.
tips: Could I have a receipt, please?
